chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Bail A. No. S- 1237 of 2015 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 28.03.2016 For Hearing Mr. Gulab Khan Qaimkhani, Advocate for applicant Mr. Muhammad Shafi Kashmiri, Advocate for complainant Mr. Shahzado Saleem Nahiyoon, A.P.G. Learned counsel for applicant has informed that in this matter the complainant has filed an affidavit raising no objection to the grant of bail to the applicant; therefore he argues that applicant may be granted bail. I have seen the relevant material which reflects that the applicant is nominated in the FIR with the role of making fires at the deceased along with co-accused. And as a result of such firing, three persons lost their lives. The complainant’s affidavit giving no objection to the applicant cannot be considered at bail stage. In this regard he shall file a proper application before the trial court for compounding the offence if he so wishes. Since there are specific allegations against the applicant, his case does not call for further inquiry. Accordingly this application is dismissed, however, before the trial court the applicant may move a proper application in the terms as stated above. The Trial Court is also directed to proceed with the matter and decide it expeditiously preferably within four months. JUDGE *Karar/-
